background.text:
I. BACKGROUND
ND 
 
 In several flaring incidents at Tesoro, the source of the flare gas is the Hydrocracker 
Stage 1 Stripper.  Currently, 400 psi hydrogen is used as the stripper gas and the overhead gas from this stripper is sent to the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant where is joins the natural gas primary feed material used to generate hydrogen.  During plant upsets, this stripper overhead is sent to the flare header.  In several flare causal reports, a prevention measure has been identified to redirect the stripper overhead to the No. 5 Gas Plant instead of the flare header.  In the No 5 Gas Plant the gas will be compressed and sent to the 100 psi fuel gas system.  This application is for an alteration to 
  
 S-1007 Hydrocracker Unit 
 
where natural gas replaces 400 psi hydrogen in the stage 1 stripper and the overhead gas 
is sent to   
 S-1526 No. 5 Gas Plant 
 instead of the flare header, when the normal routing to the   
 S-1005 No 1 Hydrogen Plant 
 is unavailable.  S1005 is a grandfathered source.  Because the stripping gas switch from 400 psi hydrogen to natural gas can theoretically cause the hydrogen production to increase, this application can potentially debottleneck the S1005 hydrogen plant.  Pursuant to Regulation 2-1-234.3.2, it is possible that S1005 will be modified by this application.  However, since S937 Hydrogen Plant Furnace determines the capacity of the S1005 hydrogen plant, and S937 already has a firm limit of 743 MMBTU/hr, it is arguable that S1005 is not modified.  To address this issue, Tesoro has agreed to a 93 MMSCFD (31,025 MMSCFY) firm limit on S1005 that is equivalent to the S937 limit.    Lastly, the No 5 Gas Plant is an existing source, but has never had a unique source number.  This is not uncommon in that many refinery gas plants are considered part of the source that provides the gas it treats.  For the No 5 Gas Plant, it was considered to be part of S-806 Fluid Coker.  However, wh en the S806 Fluid Coker was taken out of 
service and replaced with S1510 Delayed Coker (via Application 14141), S806 was archived even though only the ‘front end’ coker unit was decommissioned.  If it were clear in the application that S806 included equipment that was not taken out of service, then S806 would not have been archived but would have been retained for the No 5 Gas 
Application # 18752   Page 371 of 1334    Plant #14628 
 371 Plant.  However, this did not happen.  Engineering policy is to assign unique source 
number for units specifically listed in the Regulation 3 schedules once the unit is identified in a permit application.  Therefore, the No 5 Gas Plant has been assigned S-1526.I. BACKGROUND

emission_calculations.text:
II. EMISSION CALCULATIONS
IONS 
  
There are no emission changes associated with this application.  This application is for an alteration that could potentially reduce emissions by minimizing flaring.  However, the emission reduction cannot be quantified.II. EMISSION CALCULATIONS
IONS 
  
There are no emission changes associated with this application.  This application is for an alteration that could potentially reduce emissions by minimizing flaring.  However, the emission reduction cannot be quantified. 
 
   III. PLANT CUMULATIVE INCREASE SINCE 4/5/1991 
  
There is no Cumulative Increase associated with this application.

IV. STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE 
 
This application caused no changes in the applicable requirements or the compliance of the S-1005, S-1007 or S-1526.IV. STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE 
 
This application caused no changes in the applicable requirements or the compliance of the S-1005, S-1007 or S-1526.

conditions.text:
V. CONDITIONS
NS 
 
The following conditions will be imposed for S1005: 
 Condition #  24321  Application 18949 Flaring Prevention Measure Hydrocracker Stage 1 Stripper Overhead Reroute to No 5 Gas Plant S1007 Hydrocracker Unit S1005 No 1 Hydrogen Plant S1526 No 5 Gas Plant  1. The Owner/Operator shall operate S-1005 only when the hydrogen production does 
not exceed 93 MMSCF for each day or 31,025 MMSCF for each year.  (Basis:  Cumulative Increase) 
2. The Owner/Operator shall maintain daily hydrogen productions records for S1005 to 
demonstrate compliance with Part 1 above. (Basis:  Recordkeeping)V. CONDITIONS
NS 
 
The following conditions will be imposed for S1005: 
 Condition #  24321  Application 18949 Flaring Prevention Measure Hydrocracker Stage 1 Stripper Overhead Reroute to No 5 Gas Plant S1007 Hydrocracker Unit S1005 No 1 Hydrogen Plant S1526 No 5 Gas Plant  1. The Owner/Operator shall operate S-1005 only when the hydrogen production does 
not exceed 93 MMSCF for each day or 31,025 MMSCF for each year.  (Basis:  Cumulative Increase) 
2. The Owner/Operator shall maintain daily hydrogen productions records for S1005 to 
demonstrate compliance with Part 1 above. (Basis:  Recordkeeping)

recommendation.text:
VI. RECOMMENDATIONVI. RECOMMENDATION
ON 
 
It is recommended that an Authority to Construct be waived and a Permit to Operate be 
granted to Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company for the following equipment: 
 
 S-1007 Hydrocracker Unit 
Application # 18752   Page 372 of 1334    Plant #14628 
 372  S-1526 No. 5 Gas Plant 
 
and that Permit Condition 24321 be imposed on:   
 S-1005 No 1 Hydrogen Plant 
     _________________________   __________________________  Arthur Valla     May 1, 2009  
 Senior Air Quality Engineer

blocks.heading: I. BACKGROUND
blocks.text:
I. BACKGROUND
ND 
 
 In several flaring incidents at Tesoro, the source of the flare gas is the Hydrocracker 
Stage 1 Stripper.  Currently, 400 psi hydrogen is used as the stripper gas and the overhead gas from this stripper is sent to the No. 1 Hydrogen Plant where is joins the natural gas primary feed material used to generate hydrogen.  During plant upsets, this stripper overhead is sent to the flare header.  In several flare causal reports, a prevention measure has been identified to redirect the stripper overhead to the No. 5 Gas Plant instead of the flare header.  In the No 5 Gas Plant the gas will be compressed and sent to the 100 psi fuel gas system.  This application is for an alteration to

where natural gas replaces 400 psi hydrogen in the stage 1 stripper and the overhead gas 
is sent to

 instead of the flare header, when the normal routing to the

 is unavailable.  S1005 is a grandfathered source.  Because the stripping gas switch from 400 psi hydrogen to natural gas can theoretically cause the hydrogen production to increase, this application can potentially debottleneck the S1005 hydrogen plant.  Pursuant to Regulation 2-1-234.3.2, it is possible that S1005 will be modified by this application.  However, since S937 Hydrogen Plant Furnace determines the capacity of the S1005 hydrogen plant, and S937 already has a firm limit of 743 MMBTU/hr, it is arguable that S1005 is not modified.  To address this issue, Tesoro has agreed to a 93 MMSCFD (31,025 MMSCFY) firm limit on S1005 that is equivalent to the S937 limit.    Lastly, the No 5 Gas Plant is an existing source, but has never had a unique source number.  This is not uncommon in that many refinery gas plants are considered part of the source that provides the gas it treats.  For the No 5 Gas Plant, it was considered to be part of S-806 Fluid Coker.  However, wh en the S806 Fluid Coker was taken out of 
service and replaced with S1510 Delayed Coker (via Application 14141), S806 was archived even though only the ‘front end’ coker unit was decommissioned.  If it were clear in the application that S806 included equipment that was not taken out of service, then S806 would not have been archived but would have been retained for the No 5 Gas 
Application # 18752   Page 371 of 1334    Plant #14628 
 371 Plant.  However, this did not happen.  Engineering policy is to assign unique source 
number for units specifically listed in the Regulation 3 schedules once the unit is identified in a permit application.  Therefore, the No 5 Gas Plant has been assigned S-1526.

blocks.heading: II. EMISSION CALCULATIONS
blocks.text:
II. EMISSION CALCULATIONS
IONS 
  
There are no emission changes associated with this application.  This application is for an alteration that could potentially reduce emissions by minimizing flaring.  However, the emission reduction cannot be quantified.

blocks.heading: III. PLANT CUMULATIVE INCREASE SINCE 4/5/1991
blocks.text:
III. PLANT CUMULATIVE INCREASE SINCE 4/5/1991
/1991 
  
There is no Cumulative Increase associated with this application.

blocks.heading: IV. STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
blocks.text:
IV. STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE 
 
This application caused no changes in the applicable requirements or the compliance of the S-1005, S-1007 or S-1526.

blocks.heading: V. CONDITIONS
blocks.text:
V. CONDITIONS
NS 
 
The following conditions will be imposed for S1005: 
 Condition #  24321  Application 18949 Flaring Prevention Measure Hydrocracker Stage 1 Stripper Overhead Reroute to No 5 Gas Plant S1007 Hydrocracker Unit S1005 No 1 Hydrogen Plant S1526 No 5 Gas Plant  1. The Owner/Operator shall operate S-1005 only when the hydrogen production does 
not exceed 93 MMSCF for each day or 31,025 MMSCF for each year.  (Basis:  Cumulative Increase) 
2. The Owner/Operator shall maintain daily hydrogen productions records for S1005 to 
demonstrate compliance with Part 1 above. (Basis:  Recordkeeping)

IONS 
  
There are no emission changes associated with this application.  This application is for an alteration that could potentially reduce emissions by minimizing flaring.  However, the emission reduction cannot be quantified.

ANCE
